Notice Of Motion And Motion By Respondent Marie Villanueva For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Mar-19-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Henry Glass (Counsel for Marie Villanueva, Petitioner); Donna Quan (Counsel for Hector Gonzalez, Personal Representative).

After expressing concern as to the want of a reply by Mr. Glasser (who advised the Court that he did, in fact, file a reply) and after announcing its tentative decision (to wit: deny the Motion as to all causes of action except Cause 3 - Malicious Prosecution, would be granted without leave to amend), the Court and counsel engaged in extensive discussion as to the merits of the Motion, including the contention that the Petition failed to state facts sufficient to support a transmutation of the real property from joint tenancy to tenancy in common, whether the Court had subject matter jurisdiction of the equitable claims, and to what extent there were conflicting allegations in the Petition.

After further discussion, the Court took the matter under submission with Mr. Glasser to submit a copy of his reply today as well as a proposed Order.

Judicial Office: Dorothy L. McMath, Commissioner; Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR #11722; Bailiff: Deputy Rigo Guitron; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Court Examiner: None.

Petition To Determine Title To And Require Transfer Of Real & Personal Property To Estate
Off Calendar Dec-06-2006 Continued To Mar-06-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Henry Glasser appearing for respondent Marie Vallanueva. Mike Bassi appearing for petitioner Hector Gonzalez. Trial will take six days. Matter is referred to the Presiding Judge to set for trial. Order signed. Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip.
Both counsel returned to court at the end of the calendar. Mr. Bassi requested for referral to mediation. This matter is too large for pro bono mediation. Court is ordering parties to go to private mediation. Matter is continued to 3/6/07 for status. Trial is set on 4/16/07.

MINI MINUTES: No appearance made by anyone. Court's Tentative Ruling stands. Order signed. Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Helen Chin CSR #3856. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Mizota.

Mr. Tran is requesting for a 90 day continuance. Mr. Glasser agrees. Matter is continued to 12/6 for status. Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Teena Paxton, CSR #1558. Clerk: Ella Yip.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documentsand For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-07-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Petitioner Hector Gonzalez's motion to compel production of documents is granted. The Court has read and considered the late-filed opposition. Defendant is ordered to produce responsive documents within 15 days of notice of this order. The request for monetary sanctions is denied. Prevailing party to submit form of order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
